title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Our procedure is to locate records, review them for redactions, and then prepare them for your inspection and/or copying. If we believe the records you seek may, possibly, be furnished by another county department, your request will be forwarded to the Yakima County Public Records Officer for processing. You will be notified of any such update. 
We reasonably believe the first installment of responsive records to this request will be available approximately 04/10/2026. Yakima County will provide an installment and or an update every two weeks until this request is completed. If records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly.  Conversely, if the Sheriff's Office encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you promptly with a revised estimated date.

title: Wayne County PA Courthouse (Openrecords@waynecountypa.gov)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Wayne County PA Courthouse (Openrecords@waynecountypa.gov)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All current, fully executed Collective Bargaining Agreements/ Union Contracts between
Wayne County and any all-labor unions or bargaining units representing county employees.
This request includes, but is not limited to, contracts covering:
Court-Related Employees (e.g., Probation and Parole, Domestic Relations)Sheriffs/ Jail Deputies
Human Services/ Children and Youth Services
Any general county employee bargaining units or clerical units.
Any active MOUs or MOAs or formal contract extensions for any of the aforementioned
bargaining units currently operating under an expired contract while renegotiations are
pending.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

George Klein

communications.subject: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Wayne County PA Courthouse (Openrecords@waynecountypa.gov)
communications.communication:
Mr. Klein,

I am in receipt of your Right To Know Request received via email on May 6, 2026.
Your request has been approved and all current union contracts are attached. We only have a Corrections Officer contract, a Prison Sgts contract, a County Detectives Contract and a Probation/Domestic Relations Contract. All are attached. These are the only responsive records we have to your request.


To appeal this response, you must file one in writing within 15 business days of its emailing date pursuant to Section 1101 of the Pennsylvania Right-to-Know Law. Appeals should be sent to the Office of Open Records, 333 Market St., Harrisburg, PA 17126. Or you may file an appeal through the OOR's online appeal portal<https://www.openrecords.pa.gov/Appeals/AppealForm.cfm> form.  Please be advised that this correspondence will serve to close this record with our office as permitted by law.



Sincerely,
Andrew


Andrew M. Seder
Chief Clerk of Wayne County/Open Records Officer
925 Court St.
Honesdale, PA 18431
570-253-5970, ext. 1301
www.waynecountypa.gov

communications.subject: Your Request 20261120 - K9 UNIT RECORD (SPARKS POLICE DEPARTMENT) is completed
communications.communication:
Good day, 
 
Your request: 20261120 - K9 UNIT RECORD (SPARKS POLICE DEPARTMENT) is now completed. 
 
 The record(s) you have been provided do not contain any documentation from other agencies or any confidential information.  Sparks Police Department  is required to redact or remove all personal identifying information (NRS 603A.040), proprietary information (NRS 333.335), information from the National Crime Information Center (28 CFR § 50.12(b); USC 28 § 534(f)(1); CJIS Security Policy Section 4.2.3), information about ongoing criminal investigations (NRS 199.520; Donrey of Nevada v. Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630, 798 P.2d 144 (1990) ), information about victims of crimes (Nevada Constitution, Article 1, Section 23; NRS 178.5691); and information relating to individuals’ private interests such that disclosure to the public would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of privacy (Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Review-Journal, 134 Nev. 700, 429 P.3d 313 (2018)). 
 
 If your request contained any of the above information, it has been redacted or removed as required by law. 
 
Electronic records are available here for the next 60 days: 
https://app.sharebase.com/#/folder/146148/share/242-h5U9F-EY6AWcSMf28h4DQcBxN1k 
 
If you receive “Invalid Source” when attempting to view a document, download the document and open it in a separate window. 
 
For non-electronic records, please refer to the attached Record Summary to schedule an appointment to review the records. 
 
Best regards. 
 
This is an automated email, please do not reply.

title: Michael Kelly solar farm settlement

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Kelly solar farm settlement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any settlements between the City of Detroit and Michael Kelly -- or companies associated with Michael Kelly -- that resulted from eminent domain lawsuits around the city's neighborhood solar initiative.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Fw: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Kelly solar farm settlement
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on December 4, 2025. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, December 5, 2025.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before December 30, 2025.

Your request is being handled by Amanda Kelly. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Amanda Kelly at kellya@detroitmi.gov<mailto:kellya@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Kelly solar farm settlement
communications.communication:

title: Death Investigation Records: Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Death Investigation Records: Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding the death of Alireza Pahlavi (DOB: April 28, 1966), which occurred on or about January 4, 2011, at 100 West Newton St, Boston, MA.

Specifically, I am requesting copies of:

1. The full Police Incident Report (Form 1.1 or equivalent).

2. Any notes regarding a "Last Will and Testament" or "Suicide Note" found at the scene.

3. The Cremation Authorization Form signed by the next of kin.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
To the Supervisor of Records:

I am appealing the response from the Boston Police Department regarding request #B003701-122825. The Department claimed they were "unable to find responsive documents" regarding the death investigation of Alireza Pahlavi (Jan 4, 2011).

This claim constitutes a "constructive denial" and is factually contested for the following reasons:

Public Record of Police Presence: On Jan 4, 2011, major news outlets (CNN, BBC, Boston Globe) reported that Boston Police officers physically responded to the scene at 100 West Newton St. to investigate the death.

Mandatory Reporting: BPD procedure mandates the filing of an "Incident Report" (Form 1.1) for any unattended death or suicide. It is procedurally impossible for officers to respond to a high-profile death without generating a single record.

Improper Search: Claiming "no records exist" suggests an inadequate search of the archives or that records were removed without citing the proper exemption.

Remedy Sought: I request that you order the Boston Police Department to conduct a renewed, thorough search for the Incident Report and, if the records were transferred to federal agencies (e.g., FBI), to provide a written statement confirming this transfer.

communications.communication:
Subject: SUPPLEMENTAL EVIDENCE: Public Record of Forensic Details - Appeal SPR26/0176

To the Supervisor of Records (Attn: Clare Eberman, Esq.):

I am submitting a contemporary legal analysis authored by Larry Klayman (former DOJ prosecutor), published shortly after the death of Alireza Pahlavi.

In this document, Mr. Klayman references specific forensic details regarding the weapon (shotgun) and the ballistic impossibility of the official narrative (simultaneous discharge of both barrels).

The existence of such detailed public discussion by a legal expert contradicts the Boston Police Department's claim that "no responsive records" exist. It is implausible that third-party legal experts possess more details about the crime scene than the police department's own archives. This suggests BPD's search was inadequate or records have been improperly removed.

Please consider this evidence in your determination.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

Dear Attorney O'Donnell,

Re: Appeal SPR26/0176 (Boston Police Dept. Ref # B003701-122825)

I am writing to follow up on the Supervisor of Records' order dated February 2, 2026.
In your last correspondence, you explicitly stated: "The Department... will provide a response by February 13, 2026."

Today is February 19, and I have not received the mandated records or a valid update. The Department is now in violation of the Supervisor's order and its own committed deadline.

Please provide the requested records (Incident Report & Scene Notes) as soon as possible. If I do not receive a response or a valid legal justification by the end of the business day on Monday (February 23), I will be required to notify the Supervisor of Records of the Department's noncompliance and constructive denial of public records.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am writing to formally appeal the constructive denial and continued non-compliance by the Boston Police Department regarding my public records request (BPD Ref # B003701-122825).

On February 2, 2026, your office issued a clear order (SPR26/0176) directing the Department to review its files and respond. Following your order, Attorney Christine O'Donnell explicitly promised a response by February 13, 2026. The Department failed to meet its own deadline.

I subsequently granted them a good-faith extension until the end of the business day on Monday, February 23, 2026. As of today, February 24, the Department has completely ignored this final deadline. They have provided no responsive records, no valid exemption claims, and no request for further extension.

The Department is now in open defiance of the public records law and your specific administrative order. 

I respectfully request that you issue a new determination finding the Department in violation and immediately refer this matter to the Office of the Attorney General for enforcement action. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.communication:
Subject: Failure to Comply with SPR Order - Request for Referral to the Attorney General

Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am writing to formally report that the Boston Police Department (BPD) has failed to comply with your recent determination regarding this Public Records request.

In your determination, you ordered the BPD to provide the responsive records within 10 days. That deadline has now officially passed, and the BPD has failed to produce any records, nor have they provided a legally sufficient explanation for their delay.

Because the Boston Police Department is now in direct violation of the Massachusetts Public Records Law and your explicit order, I respectfully request that your office refer this matter to the Office of the Attorney General for enforcement, pursuant to 950 CMR 32.09.

Thank you for your continued assistance in ensuring transparency and upholding the law.

Sincerely,
Iran Archive

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request #B003701-122825
communications.communication:
Subject: Appeal - Inadequate Search by Boston Police Department (Ref: B003701-122825 / SPR26/0622)

Dear Supervisor of Records,

I am formally appealing the April 27, 2026 response from the Boston Police Department (BPD) regarding my public records request (B003701-122825) for the death investigation records of Alireza Pahlavi (January 4, 2011).

After months of delay and an explicit order from your office (SPR26/0622), BPD has now responded by claiming they were unable to find any responsive records.

This claim is procedurally impossible and constitutes a bad-faith failure to conduct an adequate search under the Public Records Law. The subject died of a gunshot wound (ruled suicide) at 100 West Newton St, Boston. BPD officers and detectives responded to the scene and conducted an investigation. It is a fundamental, non-negotiable law enforcement mandate to generate, at minimum, a Police Incident Report (Form 1.1) and an evidence log for any unnatural death involving a firearm.

BPD's assertion that absolutely no records exist for a highly publicized death investigation in their jurisdiction defies standard police protocols. This is a transparent administrative tactic designed to technically close the request while continuing to physically suppress the death scene evidence.

I respectfully request that the SPR:

Order the Boston Police Department to provide a detailed, written explanation of their search methodology, including the specific databases queried, the search terms used, and the identity of the personnel who conducted the search.

Compel BPD to explain the physical absence of mandatory incident reports for a confirmed 2011 firearm fatality.

Should BPD fail to provide a legally sufficient search or continue this obstruction, I request that your office formally refer this matter to the Attorney General's Division of Open Government for active enforcement.

Thank you.
Iran Archive

communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/08/2026 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of December 28, 2025., Reference # B003701-122825: SPR26/1593 Dear Iran Archive, This letter is the response of the Boston Police Department ("Department") to your appeal to the Supervisor of Public Records, SPR26/1593.   This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the Department.  See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records concerning responses to public records requests.  950 CMR 32.04(5). Specifically you requested: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Regarding the death of Alireza Pahlavi (DOB: April 28, 1966), which occurred on or about January 4, 2011, at 100 West Newton St, Boston, MA. 

Specifically, I am requesting copies of: 

1. The full Police Incident Report (Form 1.1 or equivalent). 

2. Any notes regarding a "Last Will and Testament" or "Suicide Note" found at the scene. 

3. The Cremation Authorization Form signed by the next of kin. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

Thank you. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Iran Archive 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2165468/ We have reviewed our files and have located an incident report that corresponds to your request. To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Glenview Police Department Illinois)

communications.communication:
RE: Flock Safety ALPR Audits

To the FOIA Officer of Glenview Police Department Illinois,

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated.

Best,
David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Glenview Police Department Illinois)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Glenview is claiming exemption 7(1)(d-5) but that only applies to law enforcement records contained in a shared system "if the law enforcement agency that is the recipient of the request did not create the record, did not participate in or have a role in any of the events which are the subject of the record, and only has access to the record through the shared electronic record management system."  

Glenview clearly had a role in the events that are the subject of the record because the requested data pertains to searches of Glenview's data contained in the Flock system.  Furthermore, Glenview should have possession and control of its data contained in the Flock System, which is exportable without requiring the creation of a new record.  Because it is capable of querying and exporting the data, which is in Glenview's full possession and control, Glenview is the creator of the responsive records and Exemption 7(1)(d-5) plainly does not apply.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated.

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: CPRA Request
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Amador County Sheriff's Office received your request for records pertaining to several available audits from Flock, namely the Network Audit, Organizational Audit, and the Event Log, dating back to January of 2023.  As relatively new users of the system the Sheriff's Office only has audit records dating back to 2025.

The Organizational Audit and Network Audit are considered investigatory records and are therefore exempt from disclosure pursuant to 7923.600(a) GC.

While the Network Audit contains investigatory record information in its raw form, we were able to redact the information that is not subject to disclosure.  The remaining information has been attached in a spreadsheet.

If you have any questions you are welcome to call me at the number listed below.

Sincerely,

Jarret


Undersheriff Jarret Benov
Amador County Sheriff's Office
700 Court Street
Jackson CA 95642
209-223-6542 (Desk)
209-223-1609 (FAX)

title: 2015-03-05 911 Suicide Welfare Check @ 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602 (Los Angeles Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: 2015-03-05 911 Suicide Welfare Check @ 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602 (Los Angeles Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to a welfare check (including any suicide welfare check) occurring on or about March 5th, 2015, at 10533 Kling St. Toluca Lake, CA, 91602, involving Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis. Incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Winnetka Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2025 (Winnetka Police Department)
communications.communication:
RE: Flock Safety ALPR Audits

To the FOIA Officer of Winnetka Police Department,

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated.

Best,
David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.communication:
Hello, 

I am reaching out in an attempt to confer to resolve this FOIA response. I am withdrawing items one and three of my request.  Furthermore, I am limiting the scope of the search for item two of the request to all data pertaining to federal agencies or law enforcement agencies located in any of the following states:

- Alabama
- Arkansas
- Idaho
- Indiana
- Kentucky
- Louisiana
- Mississippi
- Oklahoma
- South Dakota
- Tennessee
- Texas
- West Virginia

Please confirm receipt of my narrowed FOIA request and issue a response within five business days.

Thank you.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype. 3. Data Sharing a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery. Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires is anticipated. Best, David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs” You initially submitted this Request on May 22, 2025, seeking certain Village public records. On May 30, 2025, the Village sent you a notice inviting you to meet and confer regarding the Request. On June 6, 2025, the Village partially released records responsive to your Request and partially denied the Request. On June 10th you sent a narrowed Request and on June 18th the Village partially responded to your Request and closed the Request on June 18, 2025. On April 9, 2026, you messaged seeking to narrow this Request. Because the Request was previously responded to and closed, the Village will not further narrow the Request. However, if you wish to seek a narrower set of records, you may submit a new FOIA request describing the records you are seeking, and the Village will review and respond to that new request in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Village of Winnetka.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #26-333
communications.communication:
Hello I called over and left a voicemail with Records Supervisor Kim Hodge. In the last letter sent on 4/30/2026 it was indicated that:

“The records the Village identified contain over 2 million lines of entries that are potentially responsive to your request, which would need to be individually reviewed to determine what information is exempt from disclosure. “

That’s a lot of searches on Winnetka’s Flock Safety ALPR surveillance data from states that criminalize abortion. How many lines of network searches on your data is appropriate for a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request so as to not burden the Village of Winnetka under FOIA exemption 5 ILCS140/3(g)?  This will help me better understand how to further narrow my request for accountability operator use logs of Winnetka’s surveillance system. Thanks

title: 2023 May-Sep Reports & Records Upstate NY (Chenango County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: 2023 May-Sep Reports & Records Upstate NY (Chenango County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records involving Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis from May 2023 to September 2023, including, but not limited to: incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin

title: Contract for Roslindale Square Transportation Plan

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Contract for Roslindale Square Transportation Plan
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Whom It May Concern:
A Request for Proposals was issued titled “Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797.” 
I hereby request the following records in regard to this contract, that pertain to data gathering, analysis, and community engagement conducted for Roslindale Square by the Open Box, Bowman Consulting Group, KMDG, the Boston Transportation Department and any others whose work is covered by this contract since January 1, 2024:
I -Reports and Deliverables
A. Any reports, including but not limited to:
1. Survey instruments, responses, and tabulated data summaries
2. Interim or final reports
3. Memos
4. Slide presentations
5. Findings prepared by Open Box, BTD, or other consultants related to Roslindale Square
II - Public Participation Records
A. All materials and data associated with public engagement efforts, including but not limited to:
1. Invitations, flyers, agendas, meeting minutes, slide decks, recordings, and handouts
2. Screening criteria and methods
3. Focus group interview guide
4. Individual interview guide
5. Number of individual participants and how identified
6. Number of organizational participants and how identified
7. Demographic breakdowns of participants (where collected), including but not limited to:
a. Age
b. Race/ethnicity
c. Language
d. Neighborhood
e. Whether or not a business owner, and business information including but not limited to:
i. Type of business
ii. Business address
iii. Duration of business presence in Roslindale Square
8. Survey or feedback responses categorized by:
a. Demographic information
b. Geographic information
c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no)
III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement
IV - Organizational Participation
A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to:
1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates
2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.)
3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available)
4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations
V - Correspondence
A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss
Consultant Engagement and Contracts
A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment
VI -Data Collection and Analysis
A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to:
1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata
2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models)

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rosaline Russ

communications.subject: City Public Records Request :: R003877-072925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
09/10/2025 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 29, 2025, Reference #R003877-072925 
Dear Rosaline Russ, 
The City of Boston (City) has received your request for public records. This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the City. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law , p. 32, n.115. It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records with respect to responses to public records requests. 950 CMR 32.04(5). Specifically, you stated: 
To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 
Whom It May Concern: A Request for Proposals was issued titled “Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797.” I hereby request the following records in regard to this contract, that pertain to data gathering, analysis, and community engagement conducted for Roslindale Square by the Open Box, Bowman Consulting Group, KMDG, the Boston Transportation Department and any others whose work is covered by this contract since January 1, 2024: I -Reports and Deliverables A. Any reports, including but not limited to: 1. Survey instruments, responses, and tabulated data summaries 2. Interim or final reports 3. Memos 4. Slide presentations 5. Findings prepared by Open Box, BTD, or other consultants related to Roslindale Square II - Public Participation Records A. All materials and data associated with public engagement efforts, including but not limited to: 1. Invitations, flyers, agendas, meeting minutes, slide decks, recordings, and handouts 2. Screening criteria and methods 3. Focus group interview guide 4. Individual interview guide 5. Number of individual participants and how identified 6. Number of organizational participants and how identified 7. Demographic breakdowns of participants (where collected), including but not limited to: a. Age b. Race/ethnicity c. Language d. Neighborhood e. Whether or not a business owner, and business information including but not limited to: i. Type of business ii. Business address iii. Duration of business presence in Roslindale Square 8. Survey or feedback responses categorized by: a. Demographic information b. Geographic information c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no) III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement IV - Organizational Participation A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to: 1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates 2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.) 3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available) 4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations V - Correspondence A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss Consultant Engagement and Contracts A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment VI -Data Collection and Analysis A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to: 1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata 2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models) 
I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 
I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 
A public records request must reasonably describe the records sought. G. L. c. 66, § 10(a)(i). In reviewing your request, we determined that your request is currently overly broad and vague and we would have to perform an unreasonable search to fulfill your request. Specifically, you requested records “in regard to” the Roslindale and Maverick Squares Transportation Action Plan and Design, Boston Transportation Department, Nicolos Gove EV00014797 contract], that “pertain to” data gathering, analysis, and community engagement since January 1, 2024. 
A preliminary search in city databases yielded an overwhelming number of results. 
Furthermore, without any email addresses, Public Records would not be able to perform a narrowed search. 
In order for the City to fulfill your request, we ask that you narrow the scope of what you are seeking by September 24, 2025. The Public Records team would be happy to help narrow your search.  Requesters typically narrow their request by identifying a more specific date range or a more specific type of record. You may respond to this message on GovQA, email publicrecords@boston.gov, or call us at 617-635-4037 to discuss further. Sincerely,   
   
 Nia Platt ( she/her 
 Public Records Co-Op 
 Boston City Hall | Boston, MA 02201 
 617-635-3268

Good Afternoon,

I believe there may be a mix-up regarding my request. I originally filed my request to the Boston Transportation Department. After waiting awhile, I was advised to file with the Department of Procurement. 

At present and moving forward, I am pursuing this matter via the Boston Transportation Department as the BTD received the decision from the Secretary of the Commonwealth.

 On September 16, 2025, I sent an email to the Procurement Department notifying you of this matter, as detailed below.

From my September 16, 2025 email:
Good morning,
Please see the attached letter from the Secretary of the Commonwealth regarding a similar request to the Boston Transportation Department. They state:

"Accordingly, the City is ordered to provide Ms. Russ with a response to her request, provided in a manner consistent with this order, the Public Records Law and its Regulations within ten (10) business days. A copy of any such response must be provided to this office. It is preferable to send an electronic copy of the response to this office at pre@sec.state.ma.us
. Ms. Russ may appeal the substantive nature of the City’s supplemental response within ninety (90) days. See 950 C.M.R. 32.08(1)."

I look forward to receiving the requested documents.

Yours,
Rosaline Russ

 I would appreciate your acknowledgement that this request has been properly forwarded to the Boston Transportation Department. Could you please confirm that you are looking to the Boston Transportation Department to fulfill my request? The contact person for the BTD is Nia Platt *(she/her), Public Records Co-Op, Boston City Hall | Boston, MA 02201 617-635-3268.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Yours sincerely,

Rosaline Russ

communications.communication:
Subject: Clarification Regarding Request R003034-061225

Good afternoon. Thank you for acknowledging that this request remains under the purview of the Boston Transportation Department, reference number R003034-061225.

As noted, the Secretary of the Commonwealth addressed their decision to the Transportation Department under this reference number, and the decision made no mention of any modification or redirection of the request. Accordingly, I look forward to the Boston Transportation Department’s continued handling of this matter in accordance with that decision.

At this point, I would also appreciate it if all further communications regarding this request come directly from the Transportation Department, rather than from the Procurement Department.

Sincerely,
Rosaline Russ

a. Demographic information 
b. Geographic information 
c. Roslindale Business Owner status (yes/no) 
III - Methods used to ensure equitable outreach and engagement 
IV - Organizational Participation 
A. Records identifying organizations that participated in public engagement or stakeholder processes, including but not limited to: 
1. Organization names, number of representatives involved, and event dates 
2. Role or type of participation (focus group, listening session, steering committee, etc.) 
3. Contact person (name, title, email) for each participating organization (where available) 
4. Any formal or informal feedback or written submissions provided by these organizations 
V - Correspondence 
A. Email or written correspondence between BTD staff (including Directors), Chief of Streets, Open Box, Roslindale Village Main Streets, WalkUP Roslindale, Abundant Housing, Rozzidents for More Rozzidents, including but not limited to: Anna VanRemoortel, Laura Charles, Emma Blaxter, Alyssa MacNeill, Courtney Pong, Matthew Lawlor, Benjamin Bruno, Andrew Murray, Nate Stell, Elvira Moira or others regarding Roslindale Square, particularly where engagement methods, community data, or equity analysis were discuss 
Consultant Engagement and Contracts 
A. All contracts, scopes of work, MOUs, RFQs, and procurement documentation relating to the engagement of Open Box or other external consultants for work on the Roslindale Square assessment 
VI -Data Collection and Analysis 
A. All raw and processed data collected on transportation, traffic, pedestrian movement, parking, public space use, and effects on small businesses in Roslindale Square, including but not limited to: 
1. Camera footage, sensor logs, pedestrian/vehicle counts, and associated metadata 
2. GIS files, maps, and visualizations (e.g., heat maps, mobility models) 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rosaline Russ 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-department-of-procurement-42100%252Fcontract-for-roslindale-square-transportation-plan-190286%252F%253Femail%253Dprocurement%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAJadgHYt2Lv3uW3vy6-_1WkgPU%3A1ugeDI%3A5yP-3oDo5MjMgiqd3e3sdv7yEfB_J4zZeQAAaZvizcQ The record is redacted to omit identifying information, including phone numbers, email addresses, vendor IDs, contract IDs, insurance policy numbers, and NAIC numbers, in accordance with exemption (c) of Massachusetts Public Records law. The public interest in disclosure of this information does not outweigh the privacy interest of the individual. G. L. c. 4, § 7 (26) (c). 
Further, the record is redacted to omit information in accordance with exemption (a). Exemption a, the statutory exemption, permits the withholding of records that are specifically or by necessary implication exempted from disclosure by statute. G. L. c. 4, § 7(26)(a). Here, the records have been withheld in accordance with the Criminal Offender Record Information (CORI) Act. G. L. c. 6, § 167. 
You may appeal this response to the Supervisor of Records in the Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth. G.L. c. 66, § 10A(a); 950 C.M.R. 32.08. You may also appeal to the Superior Court. G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c). Sincerely,    
  
 
 
  
 Marli Mason she/her

 617-635-4037

title: Kerry McCormack Email Attachments

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Kerry McCormack Email Attachments
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All attachments, documents, files, or enclosures transmitted by Kerry McCormack (kerry.mccormack@flocksafety.com) of Flock Safety to Sharon Lowery and/or any City of Dunwoody official, employee, or representative, on or around February 18, 2026, referenced in Mr. McCormack's email dated February 18, 2026 at approximately 9:46 PM, in which he stated:

"Please find two informational pieces to distribute to the Mayor and Council prior to the meeting on Monday."

This request specifically includes but is not limited to:

Both "informational pieces" referenced in Mr. McCormack's February 18, 2026 email
Any presentation materials subsequently sent by Mr. McCormack to Ms. Lowery, or forwarded by Ms. Lowery, to the Mayor and members of the Dunwoody City Council in connection with the February 23, 2026 City Council meeting
Any materials forwarded by Ms. Lowery to the Mayor and/or City Council members in response to Mr. McCormack's request

Relevant timeframe: February 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026
Relevant parties: Kerry McCormack (Flock Safety), Sharon Lowery, and any Mayor or Council member who received forwarded materials
I request these records in electronic format (PDF or native file format). If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019407-040926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
May 7, 2026 Miriam Bhimani RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019407-040926 Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: 1. Provide a list or records regarding all grievance settlements including settlement amounts, union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable), date grievance settled, and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. 

2. Provide a list or records regarding all grievances in arbitration including union, date grievance filed, date arbitration started, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. 

3. Provide a total list of all grievances filed including union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2026 through March 31, 2026. RESPONSE : Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. The records provided include all grievances received by the Office of Administrative Hearings only. As to the remainder of all other potentially responsive data, the CPS FOIA office has determined that it is categorical in nature as it does not reasonably identify a document for search, and the collection of this information would require the District to create records. Specifically, the CPS does not comprehensively track the requested information in the manner requested, in the normal course of business. As such fulfilling this request would be considered creating records which is not required pursuant to the FOIA. Specifically, in order to comply with your request, each and every file would have to be pulled and all the portions of information you have requested would have to be searched for. Essentially, a new record would have to be created. This process is not required according to guidance provided by 5 ILCS 140/2(c). 5 ILCS 140/1, Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4 th Dist. 1989), and Yeager v. Drug Enforcement Admin., 678 F. 2d 315, 321 (D.C. Cir. 1982). Additionally, under Illinois FOIA law, a request to inspect or copy a record must reasonably identify a public record, as opposed to general data, information or statistics to be gleaned from documents which have not otherwise been created by a public body. Accordingly, FOIA applies to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Further, Section 1 of the FOIA states in part: "This Act is not intended to create an obligation on the part of any public body to maintain or prepare any public record which was not maintained or prepared by such public body at the time when this Act becomes effective, except as otherwise required by applicable local, State or federal law." In accordance with Section 1 of Illinois FOIA, the District is not required to conduct research to answer questions or prepare researched records in response to a specific query [5 ILCS 140/1]. To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706. You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court, 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. 
Thank you for your request. Sincerely, 
Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019406-040926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
May 7, 2026 Miriam Bhimani RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019406-040926 Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: 1. Provide a list or records regarding all grievance settlements including settlement amounts, union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable), date grievance settled, and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2025 through December 31, 2025. 
2. Provide a list or records regarding all grievances in arbitration including union, date grievance filed, date arbitration started, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2025 through December 31, 2025. 
3. Provide a total list of all grievances filed including union, date grievance filed, school (if applicable) and contract issues of grievance from January 1, 2025 - December 31, 2025. RESPONSE : Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. The records provided include all grievances received by the Office of Administrative Hearings only. As to the remainder of all other potentially responsive data, the CPS FOIA office has determined that it is categorical in nature as it does not reasonably identify a document for search, and the collection of this information would require the District to create records. Specifically, the CPS does not comprehensively track the requested information in the manner requested, in the normal course of business. As such fulfilling this request would be considered creating records which is not required pursuant to the FOIA. Specifically, in order to comply with your request, each and every file would have to be pulled and all the portions of information you have requested would have to be searched for. Essentially, a new record would have to be created. This process is not required according to guidance provided by 5 ILCS 140/2(c). 5 ILCS 140/1, Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4 th Dist. 1989), and Yeager v. Drug Enforcement Admin., 678 F. 2d 315, 321 (D.C. Cir. 1982). Additionally, under Illinois FOIA law, a request to inspect or copy a record must reasonably identify a public record, as opposed to general data, information or statistics to be gleaned from documents which have not otherwise been created by a public body. Accordingly, FOIA applies to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Further, Section 1 of the FOIA states in part: "This Act is not intended to create an obligation on the part of any public body to maintain or prepare any public record which was not maintained or prepared by such public body at the time when this Act becomes effective, except as otherwise required by applicable local, State or federal law." In accordance with Section 1 of Illinois FOIA, the District is not required to conduct research to answer questions or prepare researched records in response to a specific query [5 ILCS 140/1]. To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706. You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court, 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. 
Thank you for your request. Sincerely, 
Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Humboldt County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Humboldt County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Alberto Kenya Fujimori Inomoto (26 July 1938 – 11 September 2024)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Alberto Kenya Fujimori Inomoto (26 July 1938 – 11 September 2024)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into former Peruvian president Alberto Kenya Fujimori Inomoto (26 July 1938 – 11 September 2024). His name is spelled in his native language, Japanese, as follows: 藤森 謙也. His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/11/world/americas/alberto-fujimori-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1646739-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request

If your FOIPA Number is [1195846-0] please enter [1195846-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1195846-1] please enter [1195846-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000
Case Type:
FOIPA
Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)

The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.

You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.


Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system, and the FBI receives a voluminous amount of requests on a daily, weekly, monthly, and annual basis.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Our complex requests are further divided into small (51-250 pages), medium (251-500 pages), large (501-4,999), and extra-large (over 5,000 pages) sub-tracks.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  The more potentially responsive pages a request has, the more complex it becomes, greatly increasing the time required for processing. Generally, the larger the file, the longer it takes to process.
Currently, simple track cases average approximately 230 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Complex requests in the small processing track are currently averaging 1,085 days from the date of receipt for processing, those in the medium processing track are currently averaging 1,310 days, those in the large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,010 days, and those in the extra-large track are averaging 2,370 days  .


Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.
