title: George W. Bush Presidential Library: NSC Correspondence Related to Lia, Georgia Radiological Accident

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: George W. Bush Presidential Library: NSC Correspondence Related to Lia, Georgia Radiological Accident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All information, analyses, summaries, assessments, transcripts, audio or visual recordings, or similar records included in National Security Correspondence related to the Lia radiological accident and the accident's aftermath. The Lia radiological incident occurred in the country of Georgia in late 2001 and early 2002. The accident was the result of improperly stored and abandoned Soviet-era radioisotope thermoelectric generators. This accident resulted in the injuries to three Georgian villagers. The accident happened just months after 9/11 and the government of Georgia openly asked for international assistance from the International Atomic Energy Agency (the IAEA) in locating the material. 

This incident is likely to have generated considerable national security correspondence. Georgia's government was an ally of the United States. The United States is a member of the IAEA, and the Georgian government requesed help from the IAEA. Issues related to errant nuclear materials in the former Soviet Union were a prerogative of the United States as part of the Cooperative Threat Reduction program created by the Nunn-Lugar act. In the aftermath of the 9/11 attacks, threats related to "dirty bomb" attacks were routinely reported on in nightly newscasts and in the newspapers. 

More information about the Lia incident can be found in this report published by the IAEA:

https://www-pub.iaea.org/MTCD/Publications/PDF/Pub1660web-81061875.pdf

Please conduct a search for information, analyses, summaries, assessments, transcripts, audio or visual recordings, or similar records related to the Lia, Georgia incident. Limit the search to documents related to the Lia incident created from December 2nd 2001 to December 2nd 2005. An additional interest would be if the Lia incident and the aftermath was discussed in relation to the beginning of the Saakashvili administration some years later. 

Thank you for your commitment to transparency and I look forward to working together until this request is resolved.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: FOIA Request: 625 ILCS 5/2-130

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: 625 ILCS 5/2-130
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to 625 ILCS 5/2-130 (https://www.ilga.gov/legislation/ilcs/fulltext.asp?DocName=062500050K2-130):

"An ALPR user shall not sell, share, allow access to, or transfer ALPR information to any state or local jurisdiction for the purpose of investigating or enforcing a law that:
        (1) denies or interferes with a person's right to choose or obtain reproductive health care services or any lawful health care services as defined by the Lawful Health Care Activity Act; or
        (2) permits the detention or investigation of a person based on the person's immigration status.
(c) Any ALPR user in this State, including any law enforcement agency of this State that uses ALPR systems, shall not share ALPR information with an out-of-state law enforcement agency without first obtaining a written declaration from the out-of-state law enforcement agency that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b). If a written declaration of affirmation is not executed, the law enforcement agency shall not share the ALPR information with the out-of-state law enforcement agency."

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The documents I am seeking include, but are not limited to:

1) Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

2) Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

3) Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

4) Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

5) Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data.

6) Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RTL

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

In Illinois, public bodies must respond to a FOIA request within 5 business days of receipt, with
possible extensions for specific reasons.
Initial Response Timeline: 5 Business Days: Upon receiving a FOIA request, a public body is required to respond within 5 business days.
This period starts the first business day after the request is received, not the day it is received.
You will get a response from us by May 7.

Hello,

I am in receipt of your Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140/1 et. seq.) request received by the Village of Forest on April 30, 2026, for the following records:

  *   Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130
  *   Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data
  *   Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130

Your request is partially granted. See below:


  *   Policies and Procedures: Any current policies and procedures that have been adopted to ensure compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130. -- Attached
  *   Training Materials: Any training materials provided to staff about 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - Refer to attached policy, which is based upon IL statute
  *   Written Declarations: Written declarations received from out-of-state law enforcement agencies that it expressly affirms that ALPR information obtained shall not be used in a manner that violates subsection (b) of 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - Forest Park PD only shares with Illinois Agencies
  *   Audit or Compliance Reports: Any recent audit or compliance reports that assess the adherence to 625 ILCS 5/2-130.  - Not required by statute.
  *   Records of Violations: Records or reports of any violations of 625 ILCS 5/2-130 legislation, including unauthorized sharing of data. - None on file
  *   Disciplinary Actions: Documentation of any disciplinary actions taken against individuals or departments for non-compliance with 625 ILCS 5/2-130. - None on file


You have a right to have the partial denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor, (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General.  5 ILCS 140/9.5(a).  You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to:
Public Access Counselor

title: FOIA Request – Employment Dates for Omar Torres and Stephanie Jackson

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – Employment Dates for Omar Torres and Stephanie Jackson
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I request access to and copies of public records sufficient to show the dates of employment of the following individuals with the University of Michigan:

Omar Torres of Grand River Solutions
Stephanie Jackson of InCompliance Consulting (also known as Beyond Consulting)

This request includes, but is not limited to:

Personnel records reflecting dates of hire and separation
Contracts, engagement agreements, or consulting agreements reflecting the start and end dates of their work with the University
Vendor records, invoices, or payment records sufficient to show the period during which each individual performed services for the University
Any records identifying the University office or unit that retained or supervised their work

If these individuals were not employees but instead served as contractors or consultants, please provide records sufficient to show the dates during which they were engaged by the University in that capacity.

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide all non-exempt portions and describe the basis for any redactions or withholdings.

Please provide the records in electronic format if available.

Sincerely,
Laurel Billings

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Laurel Billings

communications.communication:

I am writing in regard to your Freedom of Information Act request dated
April 16, 2026, which was received on April 17, 2026.

Due to the large number of requests currently being processed by this
office, it will not be possible to respond to this request within the
five-day period accorded by the Michigan Freedom of Information Act.
However, under Section 5 (2) (d) of the Act, the University is permitted to
extend the deadline for not more than 10 business days.

The University will respond to your request on or before May 8, 2026.


Thank you,

Matthew Riggs
FOIA Compliance Specialist Associate

Suite 3300 Ruthven | 1109 Geddes Ave. | Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1079

Please send records requests to: foia-email@umich.edu



[image: A blue text on a black backgroundAI-generated content may be

communications.subject: FOIA REQUEST 261476 response
communications.communication:
Dear Laurel Billings,

I am writing in response to your Freedom of Information Act request dated
April 16, 2026, which was received on April 17, 2026.

You requested:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I
request access to and copies of public records sufficient to show the dates
of employment of the following individuals with the University of Michigan:

Omar Torres of Grand River Solutions
Stephanie Jackson of InCompliance Consulting (also known as Beyond
Consulting)

This request includes, but is not limited to:

Personnel records reflecting dates of hire and separation
Contracts, engagement agreements, or consulting agreements reflecting the
start and end dates of their work with the University
Vendor records, invoices, or payment records sufficient to show the period
during which each individual performed services for the University
Any records identifying the University office or unit that retained or
supervised their work

If these individuals were not employees but instead served as contractors
or consultants, please provide records sufficient to show the dates during
which they were engaged by the University in that capacity.


Attached find the responsive records that were located. A small amount of
personal information has been removed from some of the records pursuant to
Section 13 (1) (a) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, which allows
the University to refrain from disclosing information that would constitute
an unwarranted invasion of an individual’s privacy. Therefore, your request
is granted in part.

Please note that within 180 days from the date of this letter, you have the
right to appeal the denial of information to the President of the
University or seek judicial review in the court of claims to try to compel
disclosure. If you elect to appeal and the President upholds the denial,
you may still seek judicial review within the 180-day period.

An appeal to the President must be submitted in writing to: President’s
Office, c/o Steve Yaros, The University of Michigan, Ruthven Bldg. Suite
3190, 1109 Geddes Ave., Ann Arbor, Michigan 48109-1079 (or by email to:
FOIAappeals@umich.edu). The statement must (1) identify the request and the
final determination by the FOIA officer that is being appealed, (2)
specifically state the word “appeal,” and (3) identify the reason or
reasons why the final determination should be reversed.

If you seek judicial review in the Michigan court of claims and prevail,
you will be awarded reasonable attorney's fees, costs and disbursements
incurred in maintaining the action. If you prevail in part, you may still
be awarded complete or partial reimbursement for those expenses. In
addition to actual and compensatory damages, you will be awarded punitive
damages in the amount of $1,000.00 if the court finds that the University
was arbitrary and capricious in its denial.

A copy of Section 10 of the Michigan FOIA is available for your reference
and review online at http://foia.vpcomm.umich.edu/foia-right-to-appeal/.

Sincerely,

Shannon Hill
Chief FOIA Officer
Office: 734-763-4167 | Fax: 734-763-1399
Suite 3300 Ruthven | 1109 Geddes Ave. | Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1079

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The administrative tracking file for the request C249856-121625, and all e-mail, Teams chats, memoranda, and records of phone calls created in the processing of that request, including attachments and related metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C259142-030826
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Welcome to the City of Seattle Public Records Request Center (PRRC). Your request was received on March 08, 2026 and is appended below. Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your email contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center emails to your Inbox. 
 
The reference number for this request is C259142-030826. You will see this number in the title of any communications about this request. 
 
You will hear from a Public Disclosure Officer within five business days regarding the status of your request. 
 
Please visit the City of Seattle Public Records Request Center where you can manage your profile and access your request. Your login ID is: 206263-79217248@requests.muckrock.com 
 
If a Public Disclosure Officer submitted your public disclosure request into the PRRC on your behalf, please login and complete your new user account by following these steps: 
 
  * Access the Public Records Request Center 
  * Select 'Forgot my Password' 
  * Enter your 'Login ID' (email address) 
  * A temporary password will be sent to you via email 
  * Login with the temporary password 
You may now visit the PRRC 'My Records Request Center' anytime!  
 
Request C259142-030826:  
" To Whom It May Concern:" 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

The administrative tracking file for the request C249856-121625, and all e-mail, Teams chats, memoranda, and records of phone calls created in the processing of that request, including attachments and related metadata. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2235420/

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: C259142-030826
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C259142-030826 received on March 08, 2026 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C259142-1 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C259142-030826. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-C259142-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net) 
This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY.

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C259142-030826
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Good morning Rose, 
Records are now available in response to your request C259142-030826 regarding the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

The administrative tracking file for the request C249856-121625, and all e-mail, Teams chats, memoranda, and records of phone calls created in the processing of that request, including attachments and related metadata. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2235420/ 
The records being made available to you at this time include all records related to the fulfillment of PDR No. C246856. We are doing a final check with staff to make sure that we have collected everything. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C259142-030826. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
We have not prepared a detailed exemption log, as only limited information has been redacted from the responsive records. The information normally provided in an exemption log, such as title, author, recipient, subject, and number of pages, is readily ascertainable from looking at the redacted records. Redactions have been applied throughout the documents pursuant to the following exemption:   
 Ex. 01A Attorney-Client            Privileged Communications. RCW 42.56.070 
RCW 5.60.060 Records reflect            communication between a client and an attorney for the purpose of seeking or            providing legal advice. 
We anticipate that the next installment of records will be available on or about May 8, 2026. 
Sincerely, 
Matthew C. Jaeger 
Legislative Department

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C259142-030826
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Rose Terse, 
This update completes our response to your public disclosure request C259142-030826. We have provided all responsive records. 
The Legislative Department considers your request closed and plans to take no further action to fulfill this request. If you have any follow-up questions or would like to initiate an administrative appeal under MDAR 17-0002 Section 6, please contact us within 10 business days, after which this request will no longer be monitored for communications. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Legislative Department

title: Private ALPRs (California Highway Patrol)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Private ALPRs (California Highway Patrol)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,

Your department has access to ALPRs owned or leased by businesses.

I’m seeking the any lists, contracts, MOUs, and data sharing records that outlines which businesses and properties your agency has access. Examples include: Lowes, Home Depots, malls, HOAs, business improvement districts. 

This information may also be found in Flock Safety or other ALPR company emails that alert the department that a business or private entity is sharing their cameras with you. 

Please search 2025 to today’s date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: [Records Center] Welcome to the Records Center!
communications.communication:

title: 2026 Brady List (Pend Oreille County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Pend Oreille County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: Body Worn Camera Policies

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Body Worn Camera Policies
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request all department policies, directives, and procedures that have governed the department's use of body-worn cameras (BWCs) since 2020. In particular, I am requesting policies that mandate release of footage to the public or outline the procedure for public release outside of public records law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jeffrey Lamson

title: Hawai'i Volcanoes Park Complaints

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Hawai'i Volcanoes Park Complaints
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All complaints regarding the Hawai'i Volcanoes National Park received through the park's email portal from January 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maia Ancho

title: Public Records Request — SanDiego Generative AI Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — SanDiego Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Officer
City of San Diego
202 C Street, MS 2A
San Diego, CA 92101

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to San Diego's use of generative AI tools by city employees.

We are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, ChatGPT, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by city employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing city employee use of Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, ChatGPT, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, and any other generative AI chatbot tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

San Diego adopted a formal AI policy in 2024, budgeted $360,000 for AI pilots, and trained all 13,000 city employees on responsible AI use. City employees are using Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini for daily tasks including drafting emails, creating slide decks for council presentations, and assigning tasks after meetings (see Axios San Diego, "How San Diego is using AI -- and what could come next," April 1, 2026, https://www.axios.com/local/san-diego/2026/04/01/how-san-diego-uses-ai-government-copilot-gemini-future-potholes-records-requests). CIO Jonathan Behnke serves on the GovAI Coalition Board, a network of over 300 government agencies committed to responsible AI use (see https://www.sanjoseca.gov/your-government/departments-offices/information-technology/ai-reviews-algorithm-register/govai-coalition). San Diego was also recognized as a 2024 Digital Cities Award winner (see GovTech, "Digital Cities 2024: 500,000 or More Population Category," https://www.govtech.com/digital-cities-2024-500-000-or-more-population-category).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address or phone number below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that for Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, workspace administrators may access usage logs through the Google Admin console. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the California Public Records Act, Cal. Gov. Code 6253(c), public records include "any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business prepared, owned, used, or retained by any state or local agency regardless of physical form or characteristics." Cal. Gov. Code 6252(e). AI chatbot interaction logs created or used by city employees in the course of their official duties qualify as public records under this definition. The city's formal AI policy and its budget allocation of $360,000 for AI pilots confirm these tools are part of the conduct of public business. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request – Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

Dear Open Records Officer,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to the following public administrative and clerical records maintained by the Office of the Clerk of State Court concerning Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243 in DeKalb County State Court and/or Magistrate Court, as applicable:
1. Deputy Clerk Identities
Names and titles of all deputy clerks or Clerk’s Office personnel who processed, reviewed, docketed, rejected, closed, dismissed, or otherwise handled filings or records in these cases.

2. Digital and Administrative Records
For each filing in the above cases, I request:
Any internal audit logs, access logs, activity histories, or other system-generated records maintained by the Clerk’s Office within any electronic case management, e-filing, or document management system used to receive, review, process, docket, store, or manage filings, identifying all users who accessed or took administrative action on those filings.

Metadata associated with filings and docket entries, including timestamps, user actions, status changes, and internal notes.

Internal clerk review annotations, comments, or administrative correspondence associated with each filing.

Any administrative records generated in the course of processing, docketing, closing, dismissing, or maintaining these case records.

Copies of all filings, docket entries, and case status records sufficient to ensure a complete and accurate administrative record, including any updates or amendments.

3.  Administrative Policies and Training Materials
Written policies, procedures, or training materials governing the processing, docketing, dismissal, closure, and record maintenance of State Court and Magistrate Court matters, including procedures applicable when a defendant does not appear.

4.  Scope and Limitations
This request is limited to administrative and ministerial records and public documentation. It does not seek confidential judicial deliberations, legal analysis, or privileged attorney communications.

If any portion of a responsive record is claimed to be exempt from disclosure, please produce all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions with redactions as permitted by law and identify the specific statutory basis for each redaction or withholding, pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d).

5. Purpose and System Applicability 
The purpose of this request is to identify the personnel responsible for processing these filings and to obtain a complete and accurate administrative and digital record of how these matters were handled and maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

This request applies to any electronic system or database used by the Clerk’s Office to receive, process, review, manage, or store filings for these cases, regardless of vendor or system name.

6. Format and Fees
If any fees are associated with producing these records, please notify me prior to processing. I request that the records be provided in electronic format, if available.

Thank you for your attention to this request. 

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Subject: Second Request and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act

Dear Open Records Officer,

This message follows my Georgia Open Records Act request submitted on 12/27/2025 via the MuckRock Foundation concerning administrative records related to Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

As of today, more than three business days have elapsed since the request was received. No response, acknowledgment, production, denial, or notice of extension has been provided.

Under the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), an agency is required within three business days to produce the requested records, provide a timetable for production, or issue a written denial citing the specific legal exemptions relied upon. A failure to respond constitutes noncompliance with the Act.

Accordingly, this email serves as a restatement of the original request and as notice that the Office is currently out of compliance with GORA. 

Please provide:
	1	The requested records; or
	2	A written response identifying when responsive records will be produced; or
	3	A written denial identifying the specific statutory exemptions relied upon for any records withheld.

If the Office contends that no responsive records exist, please confirm that determination in writing.

I respectfully request that the Office respond within five (5) business days to cure this noncompliance. If the matter is not resolved, I will consider pursuing the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General’s Office or judicial enforcement.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.communication:
Subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request – Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

Dear Open Records Officer,

Thank you for your note forwarding this request. To clarify, this request concerns administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office for Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243. It does not pertain to dispossessory filings, and the Dispossessory Department is not relevant to this request.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), the Clerk’s Office is required within three business days of receipt to:

Produce the requested records,

Provide a timetable for production, or

Issue a written denial citing specific statutory exemptions for any records withheld.

The previous forwarding to another department does not satisfy this statutory obligation. I respectfully request that the Clerk’s Office respond directly with the requested records, a production timeline, or a written denial citing exemptions.

Please provide a response within five (5) business days of this notice to cure the ongoing noncompliance. If the office does not respond substantively within this timeframe, I may pursue the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General or judicial enforcement.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Respectfully,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. , pro se

communications.communication:

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

I am writing to follow up on my Georgia Open Records Act request, originally submitted on December 27, 2025, regarding Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243, and subsequent correspondence, including my March 17, 2026 follow-up requesting a more definite production timeline and a general description of responsive record categories.

As of today, I have not received a substantive update regarding the status of this request or any information regarding the search, identification, or anticipated production of records. Given the upcoming trial in this matter, scheduled for April 6–8, 2026, it is important to have a clear understanding of the status and availability of administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

To ensure compliance with O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq. and to facilitate proper documentation, I respectfully request the following information at your earliest convenience:
	
1	Categories of Responsive Records Identified – A general description of the types of administrative and clerical records your office has identified as responsive to this request.
	
2	Search Scope and Methodology – A description of which offices, departments, electronic systems, and personnel are reviewing records for this request, including the procedures, criteria, or keywords applied in identifying responsive records.
	
3	Records Determination – Clarification regarding any records that may be withheld, partially withheld, or redacted, along with the statutory basis for any such exemptions.
	
4	Updated Production Timeline – A specific estimated timeframe for the production of records identified as responsive, considering the current stage of review.

Providing this information will help ensure the request is processed thoroughly and will allow for alignment with ongoing trial preparation. Documentation of these details is consistent with the transparency objectives of the Georgia Open Records Act and will assist in maintaining a clear record of the Clerk’s Office’s processing of this request.

I respectfully request a response no later than March 31, 2026, so that any available information can be considered in advance of the April 6 trial.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and your continued cooperation.

Sincerely,
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Final Demand and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act Request (Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243) 

Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

This correspondence serves as a final demand regarding my Georgia Open Records Act request originally submitted on December 27, 2025, concerning administrative and clerical records for Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

This request remains directed to the DeKalb County State Court Clerk’s Office as the legal custodian of the requested records.

As you are aware, the Georgia Open Records Act requires that an agency, within three (3) business days of receipt, either produce responsive records, provide a specific timetable for production, or issue a written denial identifying the legal basis for withholding records. See O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A).

To date, more than three months have elapsed. No records have been produced, no legally sufficient denial has been issued, and no definite production schedule has been provided. Repeated statements indicating that “10–14 business days” are needed, without actual production or firm dates, do not satisfy the requirements of the Act.

Additionally, this request concerns administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office and is not limited to, nor properly routed through, the Dispossessory Department. Continued routing of this request to an unrelated division has contributed to delay and noncompliance.

Please also be advised that these records are directly relevant to ongoing litigation involving the handling, processing, and administrative management of the referenced cases. Accordingly, you are under a duty to preserve all responsive records, including but not limited to electronic records, audit logs, metadata, internal communications, and any system-generated data reflecting access or activity.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby demand that within three (3) business days you provide:
Immediate production of all responsive records currently available;
A definite, good-faith production schedule with specific calendar dates for any remaining records; or
A written denial identifying each withheld record or category and the specific statutory exemption relied upon.

Failure to comply will result in pursuit of all available remedies under the Georgia Open Records Act, including enforcement proceedings, as well as appropriate use of this record of noncompliance in related litigation.

I remain willing to work cooperatively to facilitate prompt resolution of this matter.

Sincerely, 

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

Thank you for your April 17, 2026 response and for providing the docket sheets, pleadings, and Magistrate Court manual. I appreciate your office’s efforts in producing those materials.

However, several categories of requested records remain unaddressed. Under the Georgia Open Records Act, the Clerk’s Office is required to either produce responsive records or issue a written denial citing the specific statutory basis for withholding.

Specifically, the response did not address the following categories of records:

System-generated records such as audit logs, access logs, activity histories, or metadata associated with filings and docket entries
Internal administrative notes, annotations, or correspondence related to the processing of filings

Any additional policies, procedures, or training materials used by the Clerk’s Office beyond the Magistrate Court manual provided

If such records do not exist, please confirm that explicitly. If they do exist but are being withheld, please provide a written denial identifying the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71.

Additionally, to the extent that records identifying personnel involvement exist within docket entries, file stamps, or other routinely maintained records, please confirm that all such records have been fully produced.

I also reiterate that this request is directed to the Clerk’s Office as a whole and is not limited to the Dispossessory Department. Please confirm that a search has been conducted across all relevant systems and divisions maintaining responsive records.

I look forward to your response within the timeframe required by law. 

Sincerely, 
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

title: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Change Made)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) User account audit logs documenting each time a department account has conducted a search or taken another action on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. Flock audit logs are retained indefinitely and contain metadata including, but not limited to: the date of the search, the time of the search, the search term/rules, and the user conducting the search. The audit logs are recorded in a database so I ask that you provide it to me as a .csv, .xls, or other database filetype.
--
2) Audit logs documenting the access reason for each time a department account has conducted a search on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. The access policy of Wayzata PD for the Flock Safety ALPR system requires a "valid reason". If this valid reason is not recorded anywhere, please indicate as such. If there are audit logs with information regarding ongoing cases and therefore they cannot be provided, please indicate the count of the logs not provided for that reason, and provide the remaining logs that do not have information regarding ongoing cases. Otherwise, if logs contain information that cannot be publicly shared, please redact the information, and provide a reason for the redaction.
--
3) Audit logs for any other departments who ran queries against Wayzata PD's Flock Data.
--
4) The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed
--
5) If any exist, please provide all agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems.
--
6) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SFPD personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple Wayzata PD members. Please search back one year from the date of completion of this request. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide an explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld. I have already reviewed the Flock Transparency Page for Wayzata Police: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/wayzata-mn-pd

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)
communications.communication:
Hi Records Division, this is an excellent and helpful response - I appreciate the transparency. I had two quick questions about the CSV file - it does not appear to list the department that queried Wayzata PD's Flock Safety cameras. Are all the queries in the csv performed by your department, or are they by a variety of departments? Also, it appears that there are multiple queries that do not list a reason or case number. From my understanding, Minnesota statute requires this: https://mn.gov/admin/data-practices/data/types/lawenforcement/alpr/
"Access to ALPR data must be based on a reasonable suspicion that the data are pertinent to an active criminal investigation. Access must include a record of the factual basis for the access and associated case numbers, complaints, or incidents. (Minn. Stat. sec. 13.824 subd.7(b))"

Can you help me understand how your agency adheres to this statute, and whether the CSV file you listed is missing this data or whether there's another one that contains it? My apologies if I missed something, and thanks in advance for the help explaining this. I appreciate it! 

Thank you, 
Alan

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)
communications.communication:
Good morning, Muck Rock news,

This is a response to your follow-up questions regarding our first data response.

In response to the first follow up question asked, "Are all the queries in the csv performed by your department, or are they by a variety of departments?"

  *   Yes, the public search audit is our department only.
     *   Reference question #3 in initial data request; Minn. Stat. 13.824 subd. 4(b), "the agency that receives the data must comply with all data classification, destruction, and security requirements of this section."
     *   Departments that receive the ALPR data has the obligation

In response to the second question, "Can you help me understand how your agency adheres to this statute, and whether the CSV file you listed is missing this data or whether there's another one that contains it?"
Your question led to an inquiry as to why data that could be observed on the User end of Flock was missing from the Public Search Audit CSV. We were able to determine that a toggle option was missed by Flock when they set up up our Public Search Audit CSV. The only option toggled was "Reason" with the missing field of "Offense Type" toggled up; therefore, not publicly displaying.

  *   We have since had Flock change this and both "Reason" and "Offense Type" are publicly displaying in the Public Search Audit CSV


The "Offense Type" is the technical query search reason and is required prior to any query in Flock - system won't let a user query without a valid "Offense Type".

The "Reason" is a manually typed additional note that the individual User has an option to add but is not required, as the "Offense Type" already states reason for access. The "Reason" manually entered is subject to what is known by officers at the time of the query.

  *   Often these queries are run during in-progress incidents when there is limited information for an officer to add a more detailed reason or a WPD case number is not yet assigned, which is why "Offense Type" is the required search reason prior to any query is permitted by the system.




Thanks,

[cid:image001.png@01DCC80C.E4E778D0]

Wayzata Police Department
Records Division
wayzatapd@wayzatapd.org<mailto:wayzatapd@wayzatapd.org> | 952-404-5340
600 Rice St E | Wayzata, MN 55391
Fax 952-404-5359
www.wayzata.org<http://www.wayzata.org/>

title: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Project Number: 1R37CA300386
Project Title: A Single, Universal, and Digitally Programmable RF System Enables MRI of Any Nucleus
Principal Investigator: HAN, VICTOR 

Program Official: ZHANG, HUIMING
Opportunity Number: PA-25-301
Organization: UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY
NIH Institute or Center: National Cancer Institute
Project Start Date: 01-April-2026

Specifically, I request the following records, to the extent they are available and releasable:
1. The funded grant application in its final submitted form.
2. The abstract, specific aims, research strategy, budget pages, and other components included in the awarded application.
3. Any revised or resubmitted funded version of the application, if applicable.
4. All funded progress reports for the award, including each annual Research Performance Progress Report (RPPR), any multi-year funded progress reports if applicable, and the final RPPR or other closeout progress report, to the extent maintained by NIH and releasable under FOIA.
5. Any other grant application materials maintained by NIH that are part of the awarded application file and are subject to release under FOIA.

This request is limited to existing agency records. I am not requesting the creation of new records, explanations, analyses, or responses to questions.
If any portion of the requested material is exempt from disclosure, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. For each withheld portion, please identify the basis for withholding, including the applicable FOIA exemption(s).


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Sterman

title: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Camera Installations (North Canton, Ohio)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Camera Installations (North Canton, Ohio)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All invoices, licenses, contracts, MOUs, memos, reports and other agreement documents between automated license plate readers vendors sent to the North Canton Police Department from Jan 1. 2020 through Jan. 30, 2026. Such companies may include but are not limited to Flock Safety, Rhombus, Vigilant Solutions, Motorola, Genetec, Insight.

This request comes in light of the presence of multiple security cameras installed throughout the City of North Canton at various intersections and to my knowledge the usage or installation of these cameras was not a publicly discussed issue. I would like to understand the cost and how they are used to better the City.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Frank Miciano

title: Dunwoody Open Records Emails to Flock

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Dunwoody Open Records Emails to Flock
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, including complete email chains, between Kayce Lowe (Kayce.Lowe@dunwoodyga.gov) and any @flocksafety.com email address in which the phrase "open records" appears anywhere in the thread, from November 1, 2025 through the date this request is received.

I request that these records be provided in electronic format. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each denial as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72.

Please acknowledge receipt of this request. Under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), your agency must produce the requested records or deny this request within three business days of receipt.

Sincerely,
Jason

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Community ALPR Audit 2 (Bloomington Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (Bloomington Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

communications.subject: City of Bloomington FOIA Request #PD-1220-2026 Completed
communications.communication:
Hello,

The Freedom of Information Act request that you submitted on 04/29/2026, has been completed. 
  
 1. We have 0 audio logs that contain any of the previously cited "search reasons" so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 2. We have 0 internal documents or actions related to misuse of the Flock Safety system so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 3. We have no records relating to the above listed issues so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 4. Our agency does not own, pay for, or access any Flock Safety Raven gunshot detection (audio) systems so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 5. As per point 4 we do not have any Flock Safety Raven gunshot detection system, additionally legal opinions are privileged and not subject to disclosure under Sec. 7(1)(a). 
  
 6. Our ALPR policy is attached and it covers use AI in the ALPR system, our current version of our ALPR training document is attached it also covers use of free form AI, and our department general use of AI policy is attached no redactions to any such documents. 
  
 7. See point 6 and BPD ALPR policy 
  
 8. The Only external agencies that are granted the capacity to search our records are our Illinois-based county and municipal police departments. The data shared upon a search includes an image of the rear of vehicle, the date/time of the vehicle image and the location of the camera that took the license plate photograph. Bloomington PD does not allow direct access to any of our ALPR data by any federal law enforcement agency. 
  
 9. Flock organization audio reports can be located on our city webpage transparency portal and downloaded from there in excel format https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sazsX-2F0zWdtuWPB29zhvRLkj2BNuoDHXS-2FwlB-2BXFZLEpQPWRcVxHf0k58bojBiIoXNVfp-2BHdAsqIYU2V-2BnFIseE-3DcMzJ_-2BELOVn-2BVcTwu1d1ln1l0neue2DtRAg3dq19nV4xiEa5Y4gMoytik-2FY-2BEpSJCIKTwQx9S-2FqRUJ3LQsXhbo3dZBEaa8BKY7IIhJccIIvGekjAAWyxFFV2H3V9rQM0FxfVHuaKI6Oa8VrYHY0xKpMESR0oDvysUmBV-2B5mf6hCbML1i7xgokha39B-2BRwu16wC-2BhKiSeOrsbqcO967Ai-2F3AXEx6hPyZZSALGfGAQcMKCjOHSMyyWxFityNznk0qCoO35T8tulR-2FQEtAt7UMoedAqaODYeZyixznaM2UWz6RR7qcRQ24b4XkO7sC-2Bj3F0OrjDS We do not provide unredacted ALPR data exports as those records contain PII, active investigative data and other license plate information and as such those records would all be redacted Per sections 7(1)(c), 7(1)(b), and 7(1)(d)(i). 
  
 10. Written Policies on how long flock data is retained can be seen in the attached ALPR policy. We are not allowed to delete any data - the data is set up to automatically purge within the time period listed in our policy. We have no incidents on records as described of where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted. As per communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations would no longer exist as any discussion regarding this issue would of been conducted through email when we were in first discussion with Flock about their system, and we only retain emails for 3 years before the records are automatically deleted and as such no records regarding this exist today. Finally Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy has been addressed in previous answers. 
  
 You can review any responsive records by clicking this FOIA Request Number: PD-1220-2026. Please note: if records have been provided, they are not locked and can be saved to your personal device for printing or forwarding. 
  
   
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Police Department - FOIA Division
305 S. East St.

title: Quality Assurance Services - Civil Works, Interagency and International Services (IIS), and Military Construction Projects in Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Quality Assurance Services - Civil Works, Interagency and International Services (IIS), and Military Construction Projects in Puerto Rico and U.S. Virgin Islands
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All invoices, purchases orders, contracts, loan agreements, statements of work, procurement documents (but not limited to solicitation documents or notices of proposed contracts, proposed bids, unsolicited proposals, and/or documents justifying contracting without full and open competition) relating to the following specific procurement identifiers:

W51DQV25R0003

You can find the contracts SAM.gov page here: https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/54399ea7c0ce4f5aaa01d69e7056b947/view
Per the page, the documents are likely to be held under the Major Command US ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS and Sub Command ENGINEER DIVISION SOUTH ATLANTIC
ENDIST CARIBBEAN

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. I am the deputy editor for The Latino Newsletter, a new media outlet focused on Latinx issues in the United States, and any documents obtained from this request will be used in the course of reporting for TLN. (https://thelatinonewsletter.org/)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Carlos Berríos Polanco

communications.subject: RE: FOIA Request - W51DQV25R0003 - Not Perfected.
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Mr. Polanco,

Under 5 U.S.C. 552, requesters are required to provide an amount they are willing to pay for documents. Your request below does not contain that information, it is missing a dollar amount; please revise your request and resubmit at your leisure, see AR 25-55§1-2(3).

For your convenience, I have attached the Citizen’s Guide.

Thank you!

v/R
Victor M. Salas-Pellot Jr.
Paralegal Specialist/FOIA Administrator
Caribbean District
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
Victor.M.SalasPellot@usace.army.mil<mailto:Victor.M.SalasPellot@usace.army.mil>
C: 787-945-4045

-Philo of Alexandria

title: Use of Force Policy and AEL (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Use of Force Policy and AEL (Department of Homeland Security)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The current use of force policy, and the current authorized equipment list showing which equipment, including less lethal weapons and munitions, that agency personnel are authorized to use.

I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am an investigative researcher for Bellingcat, and I am requesting this information in that capacity and request that fees be waived for this request.

Bellingcat is widely recognized for its reporting, and publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance using open source material.

If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Trevor Ball

communications.subject: Your verification code
communications.communication: Your confirmation code is 231369

communications.subject: New Message - 2026-HQFO-01347
communications.communication:
"01/08/2026


SENT VIA E-MAIL TO: 200664-36314635@requests.muckrock.com

Trevor Ball
N/A
MuckRock News, DEPT MR200664
Boston, Massachusetts 02115

Re: 2026-HQFO-01347

Dear Requester:

This letter acknowledges receipt of your 12/24/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), for current use of force policy, and the current authorized equipment list showing which equipment, including less lethal weapons and munitions, that agency personnel are authorized to use. This office received your request on 12/24/2025.

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Consistent with 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, the Department processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although DHS’ goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period in certain circumstances pursuant to 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(c). As your request seeks documents that will require a thorough and wide-ranging search, DHS will invoke a 10-day extension for your request pursuant to 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(c). If you would like to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner.

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request. We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS FOIA regulations as they apply to non-commercial requesters. As a non-commercial requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free, as are the first two hours of search time, after which you will pay the per quarter-hour rate ($4.00 for clerical personnel, $7.00 for professional personnel, $10.25 for managerial personnel) of the searcher. You stated in your request that you are willing to pay assessable fees up to] $0.00. [This office will contact you before accruing any additional fees.

We have queried the appropriate component(s) of DHS for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the analysts in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

Your request has been assigned reference number 2026-HQFO-01347. Please refer to this identifier in any future correspondence.

If you have any questions, or would like to discuss this matter, please feel free to contact this office at 1-866-431-0486 or 202-343-1743."
